title: crataegus oxyacantha var. integrifolia
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha var. integrifolia, also known as the English hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree with showy white flowers and red berries. The medicinal properties of the English hawthorn lie in its flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to support heart health and lower blood pressure. The health benefits of English hawthorn include improved cardiovascular function, reduced risk of heart failure, and antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. English hawthorn can be consumed in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, capsules, and extracts, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a medicinal treatment.
